markdownRemark.html:
I have always seen these fancy websites of Designers, Architects and some times from Software Engineers, and I always thought, "man, how cool would it be to also have a website of my own". Well, one day I was checking my Google feed, and a Gatsby article popped out. It was something like "Build your own blog using React.js and Gatsby". I said to myself, "here I go, fancy-website". The article was a good approach to get Gatsby's big picture, what it is, how does it work, and why should someone pick it over other SSG's (Static Site Generator) such as Next.js (the other big competitor).

First of all, to get the main idea, a SSG is an application that builds pages based on static data and templates. The main benefit, is that the files are built on compile-time, and not on run-time. Reducing drastically the response time from the server. This translates to less waiting times for the user. Another big advantage, is that the server won't host any valuable data to hack. Since the site is rendered in advance, there is no way to inject malicious data. Additionally, it becomes quite easy to scale an application. Since the server is not rendering.

frontmatter.title: How I built my personal Gatsby website
